# Spec: v2 graph engine — refactor cc-master:kanban to query graph (with JSON fallback)

## Requirement

Refactor `skills/kanban/SKILL.md` so the board render is driven by Cypher queries against the Kuzu graph at `.cc-master/graph.kuzu/`, with a strict JSON fallback to `.cc-master/kanban.json` whenever any pre-query check from `prompts/graph-read-protocol.md` fails. The kanban skill is the FIRST graph-backed read-side integration — the pattern established here is copied by every subsequent refactor (spec context-load, impact, build, qa-review, pr-review, kanban-add). The win is concrete: on a 500-task project, whole-file kanban.json ingestion costs roughly 200-500 KB of JSON, most of which is description and acceptance-criteria arrays the board never renders. The graph carries only the fields the render needs — `subject`, `status`, `priority`, `source`, `owner`, `blocked_by`, `parent_id`, `competitor_insight_ids`, `phase` — so a graph-backed render returns roughly 150-250 bytes per task. Target: measured token reduction of 5× or more on a real 500-task corpus. If the measurement comes in below 5×, STOP and re-evaluate the schema before continuing Wave 3; that signals the node schema is not carrying its weight.

To deliver full-fidelity rendering (including the `[C]` competitor-informed badge and the Review column, which depends on `metadata.phase == "qa"`), the Task and Subtask node schemas MUST be extended with `competitor_insight_ids STRING[]` and `phase STRING` columns. This extension lands in the same task — the indexer (`skills/index/SKILL.md`) and kanban skill must stay consistent in the same commit; partial updates silently produce NULL graph fields that the kanban skill then has to special-case.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `skills/kanban/SKILL.md` has a new Step (inserted between current Step 1 and Step 2) named "Step 1b: Pre-Query Checks". The step cites `prompts/graph-read-protocol.md` verbatim per that file's Citation Pattern block and runs the three pre-query checks. A check failure triggers the JSON fallback path (current Step 1 logic reading `.cc-master/kanban.json`) and emits one session-scoped warning.
2. A new "Step 1c: Graph Query" follows. It executes two parameter-less Cypher queries via `scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py query`: (a) `MATCH (t:Task) RETURN t.id, t.subject, t.status, t.priority, t.source, t.owner, t.competitor_insight_ids, t.phase`, and (b) `MATCH (s:Subtask) RETURN s.id, s.parent_id, s.subject, s.status, s.blocked_by, s.wave`. Plus one relationship query for blocked-subtask display: `MATCH (a)-[:BLOCKED_BY]->(b) RETURN a.id AS blocker_source, b.id AS blocker_target`. On exit-code 4 from `kuzu_client.py`, the skill falls back to JSON and emits the session-scoped warning.
3. The existing Step 2 (Classify Into Columns), Step 3 (Read Metadata), Step 4 (Render the Board), and Step 5 (Summary Line) are unchanged in behavior. Only the data source switches — column classification reads from the graph-returned rows instead of kanban.json's `tasks[]` array. Board output for the same kanban state MUST be character-for-character identical in graph mode vs JSON-fallback mode (single blank trailing line acceptable; do not introduce cosmetic drift).
4. `skills/index/SKILL.md` is updated to add `competitor_insight_ids STRING[]` and `phase STRING` to both the Task and Subtask DDL, and the kanban.json parser is updated to populate them from `t.metadata.competitor_insight_ids` (default `[]`) and `t.metadata.phase` (default empty string for Kuzu compatibility, not NULL — see parser note below). The design doc (`docs/plans/2026-04-graph-engine-v1.md` node schema section) is updated in the same commit with the two new columns so the doc remains authoritative.
5. A token-savings measurement is performed on a synthesized 500-task `kanban.json` fixture (committed at `tests/fixtures/kanban-500.json` for reproducibility). The measurement compares: (a) total bytes of kanban.json read under JSON fallback, (b) total bytes of stdout returned by the three Cypher queries under graph mode. The ratio `a / b` MUST be ≥ 5.0. The exact numeric result is printed in the skill's final summary line when the `--measure` flag is passed and also recorded in MEMORY.md at the end of the build pass.
6. `--compact`, `--filter <col>`, and `--detail` flags continue to work. For `--compact` and `--filter`, graph mode is used. For `--detail`, graph mode is used for the task list but the skill falls back to JSON to fetch `description` and `Market Evidence` text per task, because those fields are deliberately NOT in the v1 graph schema. The detail-view JSON read is narrowly scoped to the tasks visible in that view — NOT a full kanban.json re-read. Alternative: the detail view may read the JSON once and merge graph-sourced columns with JSON-sourced descriptions; either approach is acceptable as long as the default (non-detail) view avoids the full JSON load.
7. Cross-mode equivalence test: on this very repository (which has a ~66 KB, ~55-task `kanban.json`), running `/cc-master:kanban` against a graph built by `/cc-master:index` produces the SAME board output (modulo cosmetic whitespace) as running `/cc-master:kanban` after deleting `.cc-master/graph.kuzu/`. The test is verified manually in this task's verification step.
8. No read-side kanban code writes to the graph. The skill does not invoke `kuzu_client.py` in any mode other than `query`. There are no DDL statements, no MERGE statements, and no side-effecting Cypher anywhere in `skills/kanban/SKILL.md`. The indexer remains the sole writer.
9. On any graph-query failure (exit code 4, malformed JSON on stdout, unexpected column count), the skill emits exactly ONE warning line per session of the form `"Graph absent/stale — falling back to JSON read for kanban.json"` and completes the render using JSON. No retry against the graph in the same session.
10. The kanban skill refuses to silently degrade: if the JSON fallback ALSO fails (kanban.json is missing or malformed), the skill prints a clear error and exits non-zero. It does not print a blank board and claim success.

## Production Readiness (mandatory — every spec)

1. **No stub data:** The graph-backed path MUST query real data from the real `.cc-master/graph.kuzu/` directory produced by `/cc-master:index` against the real `.cc-master/kanban.json`. There MUST NOT be any hardcoded sample `Task(id=1, subject="example")` rows, no pre-populated in-memory fixtures, and no `if graph_missing: return [fake_task, ...]` fallback. The synthesized 500-task fixture under `tests/fixtures/kanban-500.json` is test data — it MUST be real-looking JSON in the same schema the indexer already consumes, not a trimmed subset that hides schema edges.
2. **No skeleton functions:** Every step added to `skills/kanban/SKILL.md` MUST do real work. Specifically: Step 1b's pre-query check MUST actually run `test -d`, compute a canonical-JSON hash against the live kanban.json, and shell into `kuzu_client.py query` against `_source` — not return `true` without checking. Step 1c's Cypher queries MUST be real queries that the indexer's schema supports; the skill must not say "assume this returns tasks" and skip the actual `MATCH` statement. The token-savings measurement step MUST compute actual byte counts — not return a constant "5.0×". The indexer's parser update MUST populate `competitor_insight_ids` from `t.metadata.competitor_insight_ids` and `phase` from `t.metadata.phase`, not hardcode them to empty arrays / empty strings.
3. **No TODO/FIXME/HACK:** Zero TODO, FIXME, HACK, STUB, PLACEHOLDER, or SKELETON markers in `skills/kanban/SKILL.md`, `skills/index/SKILL.md`, `tests/fixtures/kanban-500.json`, or any script added to support the token-savings measurement.
4. **Real integrations:** The kanban skill MUST shell into the real `scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py` via Bash for every Cypher call — no inline Python `import kuzu`, no mock responses hardcoded into the skill text. Fallback reads MUST use the Read tool on the real `.cc-master/kanban.json`. The token-savings measurement MUST produce its numbers by actually running both paths and measuring byte counts — not by quoting the design doc's estimate.
5. **Real error handling:** Every `kuzu_client.py` invocation checks exit code per the protocol's Failure Modes table. Exit 2 → one-line install hint, JSON fallback. Exit 3 → "graph dir missing — run /cc-master:index first" hint, JSON fallback. Exit 4 → short Cypher-error diagnostic (first 200 chars of stderr), JSON fallback. Unexpected stdout (non-JSON, or JSON whose shape doesn't match the RETURN clause columns) → JSON fallback. No silent drop-through, no empty catch blocks.
6. **Client test:** If a paying cc-master user runs `/cc-master:index && /cc-master:kanban` on a 500-task project, the board MUST render correctly in graph mode, the render MUST complete in under 2 seconds, and the skill's summary line MUST reflect the graph-backed path (e.g., "graph-backed render" vs "JSON fallback"). If the user then deletes `.cc-master/graph.kuzu/` and re-runs `/cc-master:kanban`, the board MUST render identically with exactly one "falling back to JSON" warning. If either path produces a stale or malformed board, the implementation is not done.

## Technical Approach

### Pattern Reference

Follow the pattern established in: `skills/index/SKILL.md` (for `kuzu_client.py` shell-out conventions, exit-code handling, and DDL idempotency) and `prompts/graph-read-protocol.md` (for the citation-pattern block — copy it verbatim into the kanban skill's new Step 1b). The design doc `docs/plans/2026-04-graph-engine-v1.md` Query 1 (Kanban board render) is the canonical Cypher source — match it exactly.

### Files to Modify

- `skills/kanban/SKILL.md` — insert new Step 1b (pre-query checks) and Step 1c (graph query) between current Step 1 and Step 2. Update Step 2 and Step 3 to operate on the graph's result set when graph mode succeeds, and the JSON task list when JSON fallback was taken. Update the `--detail` branch to the hybrid graph-for-columns / JSON-for-descriptions model from Acceptance Criterion 6. Preserve all existing view logic (compact columns, detail grouped list, compact single-line, filter). Add session-scoped warning emission on fallback.
- `skills/index/SKILL.md` — extend the Task DDL (Step 3, around line 417 of the schema block) to add `competitor_insight_ids STRING[]` and `phase STRING`. Extend the Subtask DDL identically. Update the kanban.json parser (lines 78-109) to emit these two properties from `t.metadata.competitor_insight_ids` (default `[]` — empty STRING[] is supported by Kuzu) and `t.metadata.phase` (default `""` — empty STRING, NOT NULL; Kuzu's STRING column rejects NULL in some builds and the indexer already normalizes nullable-looking fields to `""` elsewhere). Update the sample DDL block in the `Database Changes` section of `skills/index/SKILL.md` line ~416 to include the new columns.
- `docs/plans/2026-04-graph-engine-v1.md` — update the Task node schema block (lines 44-100 area of the design doc — exact line numbers depend on current state) to list `competitor_insight_ids STRING[]` and `phase STRING` as Task properties, with a one-sentence rationale referring to the kanban board render. Update the Subtask node schema block identically. If the design doc's Query 1 example does not already include these columns in the RETURN clause, update it to match the kanban skill's real query.

### Files to Create

- `tests/fixtures/kanban-500.json` — synthesized 500-task `kanban.json` fixture for the token-savings measurement. Schema MUST match the real `.cc-master/kanban.json` (version, next_id, tasks[] with full metadata). Subjects and descriptions are synthesized (e.g., `"Task <n>: implement feature F<n>"` for subjects, a paragraph of lorem-ipsum-style prose for descriptions long enough to be realistic — 80-200 words per task). Distribute statuses: 40% completed, 30% pending, 20% in_progress, 10% pending-with-blocked_by. Distribute priority, source, competitor_insight_ids, phase across the population so the fixture exercises every render path. Fixture size MUST be on the order of 250-500 KB. Committed to the repo so the measurement is reproducible.
- `scripts/graph/measure_kanban_savings.sh` — shell script that takes `tests/fixtures/kanban-500.json`, copies it to a temp `.cc-master/kanban.json` in a scratch directory, runs `/cc-master:index` against that scratch dir, then invokes the kanban skill's three Cypher queries via `kuzu_client.py` and captures stdout byte count vs the fixture's file-size byte count. Prints `JSON bytes: X / Graph bytes: Y / Ratio: Z.Z×`. Must clean up the scratch directory on exit via `trap`. Used during this task's build and verification; also re-runnable for future refactors.

### Verified API Contracts

No client/server boundary is crossed. All reads go through either the Read tool (JSON) or `scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py query` (Cypher), both of which are local. Re-verified against source:

- **Query**: `python3 scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py query <db_path> <cypher>` — stdout is a single JSON array of row objects. Exit 0 on success. Exit 4 on Cypher error. Source: `scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py:94` (v2-graph-engine Wave 1 ship).
- **Row shape**: every column in the Cypher RETURN clause appears as a key in the row object. Column names are the exact strings after `AS` aliases (or the expression text if no alias). Source: `scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py:127-141`.

### Dependencies

None beyond Wave 1's Kuzu Python binding.

### Database Changes

Additive schema changes to Task and Subtask node tables:

```cypher
ALTER TABLE Task ADD competitor_insight_ids STRING[] DEFAULT []
ALTER TABLE Task ADD phase STRING DEFAULT ''
ALTER TABLE Subtask ADD competitor_insight_ids STRING[] DEFAULT []
ALTER TABLE Subtask ADD phase STRING DEFAULT ''
```

Note: Kuzu 0.11.2's ALTER TABLE support is limited — if `ALTER TABLE ... ADD` is not available in the pinned version, the migration path is: drop the graph directory via `rm -rf .cc-master/graph.kuzu/` and re-run `/cc-master:index --full`. This is acceptable because (a) the graph is derived state, (b) Wave 3 is pre-v0.21.0-release, and (c) MEMORY.md already documents zero-intervention migration via rebuild. The indexer's DDL block already uses `CREATE NODE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` which makes a fresh rebuild idempotent. Confirm the ALTER availability during Subtask 1 and branch accordingly: if ALTER works, use it; if not, document the rebuild path in the build summary and have the skill print "run /cc-master:index --full" on first-use after the schema change.

## Verification

- [ ] `/cc-master:index --full` completes without error against this repository after the schema change.
- [ ] `/cc-master:kanban` renders a board identical to the current Wave 2 output (same task set, same column classification, same priority / source / blocked markers).
- [ ] `mv .cc-master/graph.kuzu/ /tmp/graph-backup-$$/` then `/cc-master:kanban` → exactly one "Graph absent/stale — falling back to JSON read for kanban.json" warning line, board render identical to graph mode. Restore with `mv /tmp/graph-backup-$$/ .cc-master/graph.kuzu/`.
- [ ] `bash scripts/graph/measure_kanban_savings.sh` prints a ratio ≥ 5.0. If ratio < 5.0, STOP and re-evaluate schema (per the design risk flagged in Requirement).
- [ ] `grep -c 'prompts/graph-read-protocol.md' skills/kanban/SKILL.md` returns at least 1.
- [ ] `grep -cE '(TODO|FIXME|HACK|STUB|PLACEHOLDER|SKELETON)' skills/kanban/SKILL.md skills/index/SKILL.md` returns 0.
- [ ] The measured savings number is appended to `~/.claude/projects/-Users-mstjohn-Documents-SRC-LJK-cc-master/memory/MEMORY.md` under a new bullet in the Graph Engine section.

## Risks

- **Risk (HIGH):** Token savings measurement comes in below 5×. **Mitigation:** STOP and re-evaluate — this is the explicit STOP gate the user called out. If the 500-task fixture produces <5× savings, the v1 schema is not paying off and we should pause Wave 3 and widen the node schema or drop more fields before adding more graph-backed skills. Do NOT merge if the ratio fails.
- **Risk (MEDIUM):** Kuzu 0.11.2 does not support `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN`, forcing a full rebuild. **Mitigation:** Branch on availability in Subtask 1. If rebuild is required, log the one-time friction in MEMORY.md ("post-Wave-3, users on an existing v0.21.0-dev graph must run /cc-master:index --full once") and print a first-use banner from the kanban skill.
- **Risk (MEDIUM):** Read-side skills accidentally write to the graph if the refactor misreads an indexer pattern. **Mitigation:** grep-based check in the verification step — `grep -E 'kuzu_client\.py (init|close|.*MERGE|.*CREATE )' skills/kanban/SKILL.md` MUST return zero lines. The kanban skill only ever invokes `kuzu_client.py query`.
- **Risk (LOW):** The synthesized 500-task fixture drifts from the real kanban.json schema over time. **Mitigation:** Fixture is committed and referenced by `scripts/graph/measure_kanban_savings.sh`; any future kanban.json schema change will trigger a visible failure in the script and can be updated in-place. The fixture is reviewed whenever kanban.json schema changes.
- **Risk (LOW):** Row shape from `kuzu_client.py query` differs between Kuzu versions. **Mitigation:** Exit-code-4 branch handles parse errors; unexpected-column-count branch (Acceptance Criterion 9) handles shape drift. Both fall back to JSON.

## Subtasks

1. Extend Task/Subtask schema in indexer — update `skills/index/SKILL.md` Task and Subtask DDL (around line 417 of the schema block and the DDL sample in `Database Changes`) to add `competitor_insight_ids STRING[]` and `phase STRING` columns. Update the kanban.json parser block (lines 78-109) to populate these two properties from `t.metadata.competitor_insight_ids` (default `[]`) and `t.metadata.phase` (default `""`). Update `docs/plans/2026-04-graph-engine-v1.md` Task and Subtask node schema tables to list the new columns with a one-sentence rationale. Confirm Kuzu 0.11.2's ALTER TABLE availability during this subtask (run a quick `kuzu_client.py query` test): if available, emit ALTER statements in the skill's DDL-bootstrap step; if not, document the full-rebuild path and have the indexer emit a one-time "schema changed — run /cc-master:index --full" hint in its summary when the old schema is detected.
   Files: `skills/index/SKILL.md`, `docs/plans/2026-04-graph-engine-v1.md`
   Depends on: none

2. Add graph-read steps to kanban skill — insert new "Step 1b: Pre-Query Checks" citing `prompts/graph-read-protocol.md` per its Citation Pattern block, and "Step 1c: Graph Query" that runs three Cypher queries via `scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py query` — MATCH Task, MATCH Subtask, MATCH ()-[:BLOCKED_BY]->(). Wire the fallback: on any pre-query check failure or exit-code-4 from kuzu_client.py, emit exactly one session-scoped "Graph absent/stale — falling back to JSON read for kanban.json" warning line and fall through to the existing Step 1 JSON-reading logic. Update Step 2 (Classify) and Step 3 (Read Metadata) to consume either the graph rowset or the JSON task list interchangeably — same downstream classification logic, different upstream data source. Preserve all existing view logic (default compact columns, --compact, --filter, --detail). For --detail specifically, use the hybrid model: graph-for-columns + JSON-for-descriptions. Do not modify Step 4 (Render) or Step 5 (Summary) except to add a one-liner to the summary indicating whether graph mode or JSON fallback was used.
   Files: `skills/kanban/SKILL.md`
   Depends on: 1

3. Create the 500-task fixture and measurement script — synthesize `tests/fixtures/kanban-500.json` as described in `### Files to Create` (500 tasks, realistic subjects and descriptions, distributed statuses / priorities / sources, some with competitor_insight_ids and phase set, ~250-500 KB total). Write `scripts/graph/measure_kanban_savings.sh` that (a) copies the fixture to a scratch `.cc-master/kanban.json`, (b) runs `/cc-master:index --full` against the scratch dir (a temp directory, not the real repo), (c) invokes the three Cypher queries via `kuzu_client.py query`, captures stdout byte counts, (d) measures the fixture's file size, (e) prints `JSON bytes: X / Graph bytes: Y / Ratio: Z.Z×`. Add `set -eu`, a `trap` cleanup of the scratch directory, and `chmod +x`.
   Files: `tests/fixtures/kanban-500.json`, `scripts/graph/measure_kanban_savings.sh`
   Depends on: 1

4. Run the measurement and verify equivalence — run `bash scripts/graph/measure_kanban_savings.sh` against the 500-task fixture and confirm the ratio is ≥ 5.0. If it is, append the exact number to the project MEMORY.md under a new Graph Engine bullet. If it is below 5.0, STOP the build, surface the measurement in the build summary, and DO NOT proceed — the wave's design risk has triggered and user review is required before any further refactoring. Separately verify equivalence on this repository: run `/cc-master:kanban` against the graph-backed path and against the JSON fallback path (move `.cc-master/graph.kuzu/` aside and back); confirm the board output is character-for-character identical modulo cosmetic whitespace.
   Files: project `MEMORY.md` (append-only) — no source code changes in this subtask
   Depends on: 2, 3

Production Readiness applies across all subtasks in the no-stub-data sense (the fixture must be real-shape JSON, the measurement must run real queries, the skill changes must not mock any path) and in the no-skeleton-functions sense (every new step in kanban must do real work; no "placeholder query — graph query goes here" comments).
